instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Benefits of ULIPs You Should Know About
Unit Linked Insurance Plans, or ULIP, is a type of life insurance plan that comes with the unique benefit of investing in market-linked funds. Depending on your risk appetite and future financial goals, you can choose your investment funds, such as equity, debt, balanced funds or a mixture of all. This is what makes ULIPs a combination of investment and protection in one single plan.
Benefits of ULIPs
Let us take a look at the various benefits you can expect by investing in ULIPs:
1. Get the dual benefit
A ULIP is a one-stop solution for people who plan to invest their money and are also looking for life cover. Along with coverage, you can invest your money in market-linked investment funds.
2. Control your investment
ULIPs offer you the option of choosing funds as per your preferences and risk appetite. You may decide your future financial goals and invest in a fund that suits your requirements. You also have the option of switching your funds whenever you want. This way, you can manage your investment to your benefit.
3. Distribute the risk
When it comes to the investment plan under ULIP, you can create a well-balanced portfolio. If you are a moderate risk-taker and wish to spread the risk, you may also choose to invest in a combination of equity and debt and thereby distribute your risk of investing across funds and is one of the crucial benefits of ULIPs.
4. Liquidating is easy
ULIPs offer a partial withdrawal feature. This means you can withdraw a specific part/percentage of your policy to meet your essential financial needs. Once the lock-in period is over, you will find ULIP as your backup plan.
5. Disciplined way of long-term saving
People have different financial goals. For some, it may be their child's wedding or higher education and for others, it may be a trip to their favorite destinations, a business expansion, and so on. A ULIP is a simple way to achieve such financial goals. It helps inculcate a habit of disciplined saving by investing in regular premiums, which can be monthly, quarterly, half-yearly or annual.
6. Get tax benefits
ULIP comes with tax benefits as offered under the Income Tax Act of 1961. Here are the details of the taxation benefits of ULIPs:
Under Section 80C, the premium paid towards the ULIP is eligible for tax benefits under old regime of Tax. You can get a tax deduction of up to ₹1.5 lakhs on premiums up to 10% of the sum assured.
The maturity benefit under ULIP is not taxable1 if annual aggregate premium is less than ₹2.5 lakhs under Section 10(10D), for policies bought on/ after 1 February 2021 if all the conditions under section 10(10D) are satisfied, In case above conditions are not satisfied, capital gains tax will be applicable depending upon composition of Debt Equity.
For policies taken before 1st February 2021, 10(10D) benefit is available if the coverage is at least 10 times the premium if the policy is purchased after 1st April, 2012 and 5 times if the policy had been purchased even before 1st April, 20122.
In case the policyholder passes away during the policy tenure, the nominee/family receives the death benefit. This amount is not taxable in the hands of the nominee2.
In case you switch between funds for investment under ULIP, there are no tax implications3.
Reasons for considering investing in ULIP
A Unit Linked Insurance Plan (ULIP) is a life insurance policy that gives coverage for a fixed duration. If the policyholder dies during the tenure, the nominee will receive a death benefit. It ensures the family does not have to struggle through financial issues after the demise of the breadwinner.
In addition to the life coverage offered, ULIPs allow you to invest an investment portion of the premiums in market-linked funds. Consequently, you receive dual benefits, which are basically insurance cover and the opportunity to potentially invest and grow your money in available investment options such as equity, debt & balanced funds market. However, the value of your investments will vary as the market rises or falls.
ULIPs can also have certain tax advantages while being a qualified insurance product. For certain sections of the Income Tax Act, the premiums paid may qualify for tax deductions. The death benefit or maturity proceeds received may be received in full and be tax-free, provided certain conditions are met. This provides a distinctive offering set to those interested in life cover in conjunction with the flexibility of investment and potential tax advantages.
ULIPs Offer Flexibility
ULIP offers flexibility in terms of asset allocation, risk tolerance, and liquidity. You can change your choice anytime to match your goals. This helps you manage your funds while keeping your life insurance active. These plans also let you raise or lower the amount you pay, giving you control over fitting the policy to your budget and needs.
Flexibility to choose your fund option:ULIP benefits include selecting fund options such as equity, debt, or balanced funds. This helps you decide how your money is used based on your goals and comfort with risk. You can change your fund choices without losing your life insurance cover. This way, your money can follow the market or your preferences while keeping you protected.
Flexibility to change your Life Cover:One of the biggest advantages of ULIPs is the ability to modify your life cover during the policy term. You are free to increase or decrease your sum assured according to your changing financial liabilities, which helps you make sure your insurance protection matches your needs. The insurer's terms and conditions may be applicable when making changes, and it may also change the premium amount.
Flexibility to change your premium investment amount:The benefits of ULIPs include the ability to change the premium amount you contribute (within limits set by the insurer) If you are no longer able to contribute your original premium, or wish to increase or decrease your premium (depending upon your personal financial changes) you can easily do so while maintain your policy in force. As a result, you can still benefit from the advantages of your plan while managing your contribution more easily. There are still restrictions on what you can change and a minimum premium amount.
Flexibility to opt for a rider:A further advantage of a ULIP policy is that the policyholder has the ability to add riders to their plan. Riders are additional coverages for one-time payments to provide additional coverage for critical illness or accidental death benefits. Selecting riders allows you to personalize your plan to your personal needs and budget. You might have to pay an additional amount towards your premium, but the benefit of the rider is additional coverage for your plan overall.
ULIPs Offer Transparency
ULIP benefits include easy-to-understand details about charges, how the funds are doing, and your policy status. You can see where your premiums go and track your investments. ULIP plans provide regular updates through statements or online accounts. This clear information helps you know how your policy is working and what costs are involved, making it simpler to keep track of your finances while having life insurance protection.
Benefit Illustration:A benefit illustration clearly shows how your ULIP plan benefits may work over time. It explains possible premiums, fund value growth, charges, and maturity or death benefits. This helps policyholders understand what to expect from their ULIP plan benefits under different scenarios. It is provided before buying the policy to give a transparent view of potential outcomes based on assumptions.
Product Brochures and Key Feature Documents:ULIP plan benefits include access to product brochures and key feature documents. These documents provide detailed, easy-to-understand information about the policy terms, charges, benefits, and conditions. They are made available to policyholders before purchase and throughout the policy term. This helps ensure that buyers have transparent and complete information to make informed decisions about the ULIP.
Free-Look Period:After purchasing a ULIP, you have a certain amount of time known as the "free-look period" to examine the policy. You can return the plan and receive a refund of the premium, less any applicable fees, if you decide it does not live up to your expectations. This benefit adds transparency and gives you the chance to understand the policy terms fully before committing long term.
Net Asset Value (NAV):The current value of the funds that your ULIP premiums are invested in is represented by the NAV. It is determined every day and shows how your selected funds have performed in the market. ULIP plan benefits include regular NAV updates, helping you track how your investments are doing. This transparency allows you to make timely decisions about fund switches or premium changes if needed.
ULIPs Encourage Goal-Based Savings
ULIP benefits support goal-based savings by allowing you to align your investment with specific financial aims, such as education, retirement, or buying a home. ULIP plan benefits include flexible premium payments and fund options to suit different timeframes and risk levels. This approach helps you plan systematically for future needs while enjoying life insurance protection. Regular monitoring and fund switching options make staying on track toward your savings goals easier.
ULIPs Offer Tax Benefits
Subject to restrictions imposed by relevant tax laws, ULIP benefits include tax advantages on premiums paid. If specific requirements are fulfilled, ULIP plan benefits may consist of tax exemptions on death benefits and maturity proceeds. Policyholders can maintain life insurance protection and improve their financial management with the help of these tax advantages. To learn how ULIPs fit into your overall tax planning, it's crucial to review current tax regulations or speak with official sources.
ULIPs Offer Liquidity
Benefits of ULIPs include the ability to take partial withdrawals following a lock-in period, which allows policyholders to access money as needed. Benefits from ULIP plans give you the flexibility to cover unforeseen costs without canceling your policy. However, minimum holding periods and policy terms apply to withdrawals. Because of this liquidity feature, ULIPs can be adjusted to changing financial circumstances by striking a balance between the need for financial flexibility and long-term savings and life insurance protection.
Benefits of investing early in ULIP
Simply put, there’s no perfect age to make an investment. However, to get good returns, you may try to start a ULIP as early as possible. ULIPs help you in building a corpus, so the sooner you commit to the plan, the more likely you are to build a bigger one. With time, you will typically see how the power of compounding facilitates the accumulation of attractive returns over a period of time. As the premium frequency can be monthly, it allows you to save in smaller amounts and still build up a considerable corpus.
As life coverage is a major advantage of ULIPs, you also enjoy financial security against unfortunate mishaps. Apart from this, the inherent tax benefits also make investing early in ULIPs a wiser choice.
How ULIPs may benefit individuals starting a family?
A ULIP generally works better when it is treated as a long-term investment. It can offer you a long list of advantages which make it quite suitable if you are planning to start a family soon. Let’s take a look at how a ULIP plan benefits individuals starting a family:
1. Build a corpus for new pursuits
Planning to get married soon, or a new baby is on the way? ULIPs can allow you to save towards your new pursuits.
2. Secure your child’s future
Having a baby can increase your responsibilities manifold. Securing their future seems manageable when you invest in the right ULIP. One of the major advantages of ULIP is the premium waiver benefit that waives off future premiums in case of the death of the assured.
3. A back-up for emergencies
An unannounced family emergency can be troublesome. The partial withdrawal option after the 5-year lock-in can help you with the required funds.
Conclusion
From collecting money to buying a new house or planning a destination wedding, a ULIP is a suitable investment product that can help you with goal-based savings. Along with building a suitable corpus for your future needs, ULIP ensures that your family receives financial support in case of your unfortunate death.
One can choose the investments depending on their risk appetite, which makes ULIPs a well-suited option for almost all kinds of investors. As the policyholder has control over the investments, it offers the much-needed flexibility that investors these days look for.
FAQs
1. What are the features of ULIP?
ULIPs come with several distinct features:
Along with life coverage, you invest in market-linked funds
With fund switching, you can move your money
Once the 5-year lock-in is over, you can withdraw partially, subject to policy terms & conditions
Keep up with your changing goals by topping up your ULIPs.
2. What is the average lock-in period with ULIP?
Typically, ULIPs come with a lock-in period of 5 years. In case you wish to surrender your policy, you can do so at any time.
3. How does a ULIP provide a combination of insurance and investment?
ULIP is a type of life insurance plan that comes with the advantage of market-linked returns. In the dual-purpose plan, one part of the premium is used towards life cover, while the other is invested.
4. How does a ULIP offer flexibility in terms of investment options?
While the terms of each plan may vary, flexibility is one of the prime ULIP benefits, which you can enjoy in the form of fund switching, top-ups, partial withdrawals, premium redirections, etc.
Disclaimer:
IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER
The Unit Linked Insurance Products do not offer any liquidity during the first five years of the contract. The policyholder will not be able to surrender or withdraw the monies invested in Unit Linked Insurance Products completely or partially till the end of the fifth year.
ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors. The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions. Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company. The various funds offered under this contract are the names of the funds and do not in any way indicate the quality of these plans, their future prospects and returns.
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. This is what makes ULIPs a combination of investment and protection in one single plan.

Let us take a look at the various benefits you can expect by investing in ULIPs:
1. Get the dual benefit
A ULIP is a one-stop solution for people who plan to invest their money and are also
looking for life cover. Along with coverage, you can invest your money in market-linked
investment funds.
2. Control your investment
ULIPs offer you the option of choosing funds as per your preferences and risk appetite.
You may decide your future financial goals and invest in a fund that suits your

This way, you can manage your investment to your benefit.

● Under Section 80C, the premium paid towards the ULIP is eligible for tax benefits

● The maturity benefit under ULIP is not taxable1 if annual aggregate premium is

● In case you switch between funds for investment under ULIP, there are no tax

A Unit Linked Insurance Plan (ULIP) is a life insurance policy that provides coverage for a fixed duration. If the life assured dies during the tenure, the nominee will receive a death benefit. It ensures the family does not have to struggle through financial issues after the demise of the breadwinner.
In addition to the life coverage offered, ULIPs allow you to invest a portion of the premiums in market- linked funds. ULIPs can also have tax advantages. For certain sections of the Income Tax Act, the premiums paid may qualify for tax deductions. The death benefit or maturity proceeds received may be received in full and be tax-free, provided certain conditions are met. This provides a distinctive offering set to those interested in life to cover in conjunction with the flexibility of investment and potential tax advantages.
1. ULIPs Offer Flexibility
ULIP offers flexibility in terms of asset allocation, risk tolerance, and liquidity after the lock in period. You can change your choice anytime to match your goals. This helps you manage your funds while keeping your life insurance plan active.
Flexibility to choose the type of fund : ULIP benefits include selecting the type of fund such as equity, debt, or balanced funds. This helps you decide how your money is used based on your goals and your risk-taking ability. You can switch the type of funds based on market volatility and your risk appetite.
Flexibility to opt for a rider: Another advantage of a ULIP plan is that the policyholder can add riders. Riders are add-on benefits available at additional nominal premiums. Such as critical illness benefit rider or accidental death benefit rider etc. Selecting riders allows you get add on coverage over and above your life insurance plan depending on your needs and budget.
2. ULIPs Offer Transparency
ULIP benefits include easy-to-understand details about charges, how the funds are doing, and your policy status. You can see where your premiums go and track your investments.. This clear information
helps you know how your policy is working and what costs are involved, making it simpler to keep track of your finances while having life insurance protection.
3. Goal-Based Savings with ULIPs
ULIPs help in sgoal-based savings by allowing you to align your investment with specific financial aims, such as education, retirement, buying a home etc. ULIP plan benefits include flexible premium payments and fund options to suit different timeframes and risk levels. This approach helps you plan systematically for future needs while enjoying life insurance protection. Regular monitoring and fund switching options help you stay on track toward your savings and goals.
4. ULIPs Offer Tax Benefits
Subject to restrictions imposed by relevant tax laws, ULIP benefits include tax advantages on premiums paid. If specific requirements are fulfilled, ULIP plan benefits may consist of tax exemptions on death benefits and maturity proceeds. Policyholders can maintain life insurance protection and improve their financial management with the help of these tax advantages.
5. ULIPs Offer Liquidity Post the Lock In Period
Benefits of ULIPs include the ability to take partial or complete withdrawals after a 5-year lock in period , subject to policy terms and conditions. Because of this liquidity feature, ULIPs can be adjusted to changing financial circumstances by striking a balance between the need for financial flexibility and long- term savings and life insurance protection.

3. A back-up for emergencies

From collecting money to buying a new house or planning a destination wedding, a

One can choose the investments depending on their risk appetite, which makes ULIPs a
